channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ottawa commits to quicker synagogue security funding and stronger hate crime legislation
item.description: Following recent shootings targeting three synagogues in the Toronto area, Federal Public Safety Minister Gary Anandasangeree has committed to accelerating security funding for synagogues, Jewish scho...

item.title: Mojtaba Khamenei appointed as Iran’s new supreme leader amid US-Israeli conflict
item.description: Iran has officially named Mojtaba Khamenei, the second son of the late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, as the new supreme leader, giving him the ultimate authority over all state matters. His appointment...

item.title: Palmer Luckey's ModRetro in Talks to Raise Funds at $1 Billion Valuation
item.description: ModRetro, the retro gaming company founded by Palmer Luckey, is reportedly in early discussions to raise new funding at a $1 billion valuation, according to sources cited by the Financial Times. Lucke...

item.title: Gisèle Pelicot’s Memoir ‘A Hymn to Life’ Explores Survival After Years of Drugging and Rape by Husband and Others
item.description: Gisèle Pelicot’s memoir, titled *A Hymn to Life*, begins with the shocking revelation in November 2020 that her husband Dominique had drugged her for nearly a decade and invited around 80 men to rape ...

item.title: Pakistan Government Plans Austerity Measures Amid Fuel Crisis Triggered by Middle East Conflict
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif is set to announce a comprehensive austerity plan today in response to the ongoing global fuel crisis, which has been exacerbated by the US-Israel war on Iran and subsequ...

item.title: TECH Global University recognized by Google for top digital performance
item.description: TECH Global University, which Forbes ranks as the world's best online university, has received the Google Premier Partner badge. This recognition is reserved for the top 3% of companies worldwide that...

item.title: Mojtaba Khamenei appointed Iran's Supreme Leader following father's death in US-Israeli strike
item.description: Mojtaba Khamenei, aged 56 and second son of the late Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has been named Iran's new Supreme Leader by the Assembly of Experts in a 'decisive vote', according to state media. This ma...

item.title: Why real transformation in Kenya needs both roads and shared values
item.description: The article argues that Kenya cannot achieve real transformation just by building roads, dams and railways; it also needs to fix the values that guide leaders and citizens. The writer says that while ...

item.title: Jesse Jackson Jr. Reflects on His Father's Legacy, Highlights Disconnect with Former Presidents at Memorial
item.description: During the memorial service for the late Rev. Jesse Jackson in Chicago, his son, Jesse Jackson Jr., delivered a pointed message regarding the tributes given by former U.S. presidents Barack Obama, Bil...

item.title: APM Candidate Raises Alarm Over N13.7bn Alleged Ghost Worker Fraud in Osun State
item.description: The governorship candidate of the Allied Peoples Movement (APM) for the 2026 Osun State election, Adewale Adebayo, don drag Governor Ademola Adeleke over the alleged N13.7 billion ghost worker scam we...

item.title: SABC Adjusts Financial Expectations Amid Rising Costs
item.description: The South African Broadcasting Corporation (SABC) has revised its financial goals for the 2026/27 fiscal year, moving away from its previously announced target of a R1 billion profit. According to pro...

item.title: Government Assures Fuel Supply with Three Petrol Shipments Arriving Tomorrow Amid Middle East Crisis
item.description: Petroleum Minister Ali Pervaiz Malik announced on Sunday that three petroleum shipments are expected to arrive in Pakistan by Monday, providing some relief amid concerns over fuel reserves and rising ...

item.title: Bomet Photojournalist Mourned at Funeral After Helicopter Crash
item.description: Bomet County witnessed a heartfelt farewell for a dedicated photojournalist who perished in a tragic helicopter crash in Nandi County. The ceremony brought together family, friends, colleagues, and lo...

item.title: Shoprite focuses on Southern Africa and boosts digital sales amid West African exit
item.description: Shoprite Holdings, which is the biggest retailer in Africa, is showing strong growth even in tough economic times. For the six months ending December 2025, Shoprite reported a 7.2% increase in merchan...

item.title: A Stranger's Compassion Helped Me Cope with Grief on a Flight
item.description: During a long-haul flight from California to Australia, a passenger shared a deeply personal account of coping with grief after the death of their father. Already emotional from leaving friends and fa...

item.title: Raijor Dal expresses disappointment over Congress, Akhil Gogoi criticises alliance issues in Assam elections 2026
item.description: In the lead-up to the Assam Assembly elections 2026, tensions have arisen between Raijor Dal and the Indian National Congress. Akhil Gogoi, leader of Raijor Dal, has openly criticised Gaurav Gogoi and...

item.title: Iraq Faces Escalating Regional Tensions Amid U.S.-Iran-Israel Conflict
item.description: Iraq, which had recently made strides towards stability following decades of conflict, is now facing renewed instability as the Middle East war escalates. Following U.S. and Israeli attacks on Iran, I...

item.title: Nueva Vizcaya forms council to monitor and stabilize basic commodity prices
item.description: The Provincial Government of Nueva Vizcaya has activated its Price Coordinating Council to closely monitor market conditions amid rising fuel costs that could impact the prices of essential goods. Gov...

item.title: Guardiola focuses on Champions League and Carabao Cup as City chase quadruple
item.description: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola has expressed regret that the Premier League title is no longer entirely within his team's control, with Arsenal currently holding a seven-point lead and having p...

item.title: Learners in Eastern Cape Farming Communities Struggle Without Scholar Transport
item.description: In the rural farming communities of Enon and Bersheba, Eastern Cape, over 80 learners have been left without scholar transport this year, forcing them to hitchhike daily to Moses Mabida Secondary Scho...
